name: AURA Transaction Service

delivery.quic: first unidirectional stream on a new QUIC connection contains the 36-byte UUID API key

api_key.how_to_get: Contact AURA via Telegram @aurauntouched or Discord.
api_key.note: Do not commit a real API key. Docs examples use placeholders only.

tip_note: Include a tip of at least 0.001 SOL to one of the 7 Aura tip wallets. Random wallet selection is recommended to distribute load.
cost_on_failure: not explicitly documented; tip is a SOL transfer in the submitted transaction, so it is only paid if the transaction lands and executes the tip instruction

http_direct.note: Docs describe HTTP direct endpoints as lowest latency and recommend HTTP/2 long connections with keep-alive.

sendTransaction.description: JSON-RPC transaction submission using a base64-encoded transaction.

sendBundle.description: Atomic bundle submission over JSON-RPC. Supports up to 4 transactions.
sendBundle.note: Works with the same anti-MEV protection endpoints.

sendTransactionBatch.description: Non-atomic batch send. Each transaction is processed independently.
sendTransactionBatch.note: Unlike sendBundle, transactions go directly in params with no inner array.

sendTransaction_binary.description: Raw transaction bytes over HTTP. Fastest HTTP option.

sendTransaction_quic.description: Raw transaction bytes over a persistent QUIC connection.

authentication.stream: The first unidirectional stream on every new connection contains the API key.
authentication.failure: Invalid or inactive keys cause the server to close the connection immediately.

submission.fire_and_forget: Open a unidirectional stream, write raw transaction bytes, finish the stream.
submission.with_confirmation: Open a bidirectional stream, write raw transaction bytes, finish send side, then read signature or ERR:<reason>.

limitations: No bundle support over QUIC; use HTTP sendBundle for atomic bundles.
sendTransaction_quic.dedup: Duplicate transactions are dropped silently; only the first copy is forwarded.

getHealth.description: Health check / keep-alive JSON-RPC method. No API key required.

mev_protection.mechanism: Include the Aura MEV protection account in the transaction.

rate_limits.note: Docs list a rate limit error but do not publish default TPS.

notes: The .stack provider label AURA appears to refer to AURA Transaction Service / AuraBoost.
Docs recommend direct HTTP endpoints for lowest latency and HTTP/2 long connections.
Do not push real API keys, private plan limits, or Telegram-provided credentials to GitHub.
